key_findings: End-to-end platform is a 7-layer architecture: Corpus Manager → Oracle Execution → Parser → Ingest → Scoring → Analyst API → ORS/AWSEM meta-layer

Dual-store authority model is the core design invariant: Neo4j holds canonical entity graph (relationships, KRIL scores, findings); ClickHouse holds telemetry and aggregate analytics — neither store can substitute the other
Checkpoint-first write order (checkpoint → Neo4j → ClickHouse) is enforced by code assertion — not documentation — and enables full corpus replay with ≤ 0.5% data loss
Three phases (≤2 weeks each) deliver usable value incrementally: Phase 1 = DNS corpus processable; Phase 2 = HTTP positives + enriched findings; Phase 3 = analyst API + reporting
AWSEM dispatch-level concurrency caps are non-negotiable — advisory caps allow burst overcommit that causes Oracle CPU spikes and DLQ cascades
Phase gate automation in AWSEM (HTTP blocked until Phase 1 ORS green, Enrich blocked until HTTP positive gate) is required for 72h unattended operation
Bloom filter (Redis, 10M insertions, 0.01% FPR) provides O(1) pre-write dedup check — critical at 10M+ scale where query-per-write creates unacceptable throughput overhead
REST API backed by Neo4j (graph queries) + ClickHouse (aggregate stats) — materialized views prevent query latency regression as corpus grows to 10M+ records
What must be built net-new: HTTP/nuclei/katana parsers, KRIL scoring module, risk/value scoring, REST API (5 endpoints), Mattermost reporting, ClickHouse materialized views
What can be deferred to v1.1: additional Oracle workers, frontend dashboard, full Cypher graph analytics — REST API + Mattermost sufficient for v1 intelligence consumption

decisions.title: Dual-store authority model: Neo4j + ClickHouse
decisions.summary: Neo4j = canonical entity graph (relationships, KRIL, findings); ClickHouse = telemetry and aggregate analytics
decisions.rationale: Neo4j excels at graph traversal and entity linking; ClickHouse excels at time-series aggregation. Forcing either store into the other's workload creates performance and schema complexity without benefit.

decisions.title: Neo4j-first write order with checkpoint assertion
decisions.summary: Write order: checkpoint(PENDING) → neo4j_write → clickhouse_write → checkpoint(COMPLETED). Enforced by code assertion, not documentation.
decisions.rationale: Neo4j is the canonical entity store. ClickHouse records without Neo4j entity are orphaned telemetry. Checkpoint-first enables safe replay at exactly the failed record.

decisions.title: AWSEM dispatch-level concurrency caps
decisions.summary: Concurrency caps enforced at task dispatch, not as advisory limits. AWSEM will not dispatch beyond cap regardless of queue depth.
decisions.rationale: Advisory caps allow burst overcommit under backpressure. Dispatch-level enforcement prevents Oracle CPU spikes that cascade into DLQ failures across all lanes.

decisions.title: Three phased delivery (≤2 weeks each)
decisions.summary: Phase 1: DNS ingest core. Phase 2: HTTP + Enrichment + Scoring. Phase 3: Analyst API + Reporting.
decisions.rationale: Staged delivery allows early production use of DNS data while HTTP and API layers are built. Reduces rework risk by validating foundation before adding complexity.

decisions.title: Bloom filter for pre-write dedup at O(1)
decisions.summary: Redis Bloom filter (10M insertions, 0.01% FPR) checked before every write. Single-record Neo4j lookup per write is O(N) and unacceptable at 10M+ scale.
decisions.rationale: 10M FQDN corpus × query-per-write = 10M Neo4j queries. Bloom filter reduces this to 10M O(1) lookups with 0.01% false positive rate (1,000 unnecessary dedup checks per 10M — acceptable).

decisions.title: Phase gate automation in AWSEM
decisions.summary: HTTP queue activation requires ORS Phase 1 gate confirmation. Enrich queue requires HTTP positive gate. Automated gate prevents manual timing errors during 72h unattended operation.
decisions.rationale: Manual gate activation during 72h operation is error-prone. Automating gate passage via ORS signal eliminates the class of 'activated HTTP too early' or 'never activated Enrich' failures.

decisions.title: REST API backed by Neo4j + ClickHouse with materialized views
decisions.summary: Graph queries → Neo4j Cypher; aggregate stats → ClickHouse materialized views. Depth limit: max 3 hops on graph traversal.
decisions.rationale: Materialized views prevent aggregate query latency regression as ClickHouse table grows to 10M+ rows. Graph depth limit prevents full-graph traversal from degrading API response time.

decisions.title: Frontend dashboard deferred to v1.1
decisions.summary: REST API + Mattermost reporting sufficient for analyst consumption in v1. Frontend complexity deferred.
decisions.rationale: Building a frontend in Phase 3 alongside API completion creates scope risk. Mattermost + REST API delivers sufficient intelligence access for Day-1 operations.

system_layers.name: Corpus Manager (AWSEM Scheduler)
critical_contracts: Phase gate: HTTP blocked until ORS Phase 1 green
Concurrency caps enforced at dispatch level

system_layers.name: Scan Execution (Oracle)
responsibilities: Write output to temp dir atomically

system_layers.name: Parser (Controller)
critical_contracts: Malformed → quarantine({batch_id, reason}); emit DLQ signal

system_layers.name: Ingest (Controller)
critical_contracts: Write order: bloom_check → checkpoint(PENDING) → neo4j_write → clickhouse_write → checkpoint(COMPLETED)
ClickHouse: ReplacingMergeTree(version), all reconciliation queries with FINAL
On any step fail: retry → DLQ; never skip checkpoint

system_layers.name: Scoring (Controller)
responsibilities: Write-back to Neo4j after each scan cycle
critical_contracts: KRIL: 5-signal composite (http_positive 30%, finding_density 25%, status_interesting 20%, recency 15%, link_complexity 10%)
KRIL batch: 50 targets per T2 call (WO-00004 routing policy)

system_layers.name: Analyst API (Controller)
critical_contracts: Graph traversal: max depth 3 hops enforced

system_layers.name: ORS + AWSEM (Meta-layer)
critical_contracts: ORS heartbeat: external watchdog; alert if absent > 60s
Static fallback table in AWSEM if ORS fails
Alert dedup: 10-minute window per signal; suppression 30 min after reflex

phase_milestones.name: Core Ingest Pipeline
phase_milestones.deliverable: 10M DNS corpus processable; entities in Neo4j and ClickHouse

phase_milestones.name: HTTP + Enrichment + Scoring
phase_milestones.deliverable: HTTP positives identified and scored; findings enriched and ranked by KRIL

phase_milestones.name: Analyst API + Reporting
phase_milestones.deliverable: Production analyst platform; intelligence consumable without raw data access

risk_model.title: Neo4j degrades at 10M+ node scale
risk_model.mitigation: Heap+page cache pre-configured; MERGE batch ≤ 500 ops; no full-graph scans during ingest

risk_model.title: ClickHouse background merge falls behind
risk_model.mitigation: ORS monitors clickhouse_parts_per_table; OPTIMIZE TABLE on WARNING

risk_model.title: API query performance degrades under load
risk_model.mitigation: Materialized views for aggregate queries; depth limit on graph traversal

risk_model.title: Enrichment DLQ accumulation
risk_model.mitigation: Per-lane DLQ TTL; ORS alert at 500 items; Enrich concurrency auto-reduced on Oracle memory pressure

risk_model.title: Phase gate failure delays multi-lane activation
risk_model.mitigation: Gate metrics are measurable and observable; Phase 1 window builds confidence before commit

risk_model.title: Scoring batch bottleneck
risk_model.mitigation: KRIL scoring runs after scan cycle; does not block ingest throughput

recommended_actions.action: Build DNS ingest path end-to-end (Phase 1) before adding HTTP — validates foundation before complexity
Apply Neo4j schema constraints and verify before first production write
Implement phase gate automation in AWSEM before Phase 2 — manual gate timing is error-prone at 72h
Add ORS 15-signal monitoring before Phase 2 start — enrichment + scoring add complexity
Implement /api/v1/stats/summary in Phase 1 — gives immediate operational visibility
Use ClickHouse materialized views for all aggregate API queries
Defer frontend dashboard to v1.1; REST API + Mattermost sufficient for v1
Run 1h dry-run for each phase before committing to 72h operation window

assumptions: A1: Oracle has >= 32 CPU cores, >= 64GB RAM; Controller has >= 16GB RAM (32GB recommended)
A2: Redis, Neo4j, ClickHouse deployable on Controller or Oracle based on RAM constraints
A3: AWSEM supports concurrency cap enforcement at dispatch level
A4: ORS reads real-time signals from Neo4j, ClickHouse, and Redis
A5: Mattermost webhook URL available for reporting integration
A6: dnsx, httpx, nuclei, katana tools available on Oracle with version pinning
A7: REST API requires no auth for v1 (internal network only); auth deferred to v1.1
A8: ClickHouse materialized views sufficient for analyst dashboard queries without additional BI layer
